revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hashizu Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hashizu Kofun Cluster | native_name =橋津古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hashizu Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|16|N|133|52|55|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|qid=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|16|N|133|52|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Hashizu Kofun Cluster'''|橋津古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Hashizu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|qid=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2128|title= 橋津古墳群|trans-title= Hashizu Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Hashizu Kofun Cluster is located on the Umanoyama Hill, between the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} and {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|qid=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō|1=Lake Tōgō}} at an elevation of 107 meters above sea level. The cluster consists of five {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and 19 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) tumuli dating from the early to late Kofun period. The site is therefore also called the {{nihongo|Umanoyama Kofun Cluster|馬ノ山古墳群||}}. Of the 24 tumuli, 14 are covered in the National Historic Site designation.
Of the keyhole-shaped Kofuns, Kofun No. 4, which was built in the early Kofun period (around the middleof the 4th century) and which a total length of about 100 meters, is the largest, and includes a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and a cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''-style coffin. The interior was filled with red clay, and the exterior covered in plate-like stones and cylindrical ''haniwa''. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered include triangular-rimmed divine beast {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, jade ''[[magatama]]'' and jasper cylindrical beads. Kofun No.4 is presumed to be one of the earliest keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in the eastern portion of {{ill|Hōki Province|en|Hōki Province|qid=Q1053703|lt=Hōki Province|1=Hōki Province}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus cluster is located 30-minutes by car from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|en|Kurayoshi Station|qid=Q899254|lt=Kurayoshi Station|1=Kurayoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|qid=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
